arch: mamba-1 (S6 selective-scan); FalconMamba variant = vanilla Mamba-1 + weightless RMSNorm on B/C/dt (bcdt_rms_eps 1e-6)
serving_tokenizer_gguf: models/falcon-mamba-7b-tokenizer.gguf (gpt2 byte-level BPE, vocab 65024, real Falcon tokenizer — 'qwen2' family is the generic GPT-2 BPE loader that reads the gguf's own vocab)

reference_verified.forward_math: from_knot + forward_one is HF-bit-exact. Re-encoded a real trained state-spaces/mamba-130m-hf with the production encode-ssm-models.py and ran the real paris-probe-rknot binary: next-token argmax matched HF greedy at f32 AND Q8 (max|delta|=2.4e-7, cosine=1.0). mamba-2.8b + falcon-mamba share this mamba1_s6_step path, so the proof covers both; falcon adds only the verified RMSNorm branch.
reference_verified.quantization: Q8_0 residency on in_proj/out_proj/lm_head (raw-Q8 in-kernel dequant GEMV). Current 34-byte block format (in_proj len == blocks*34, not the stale 36-byte format). f32 == Q8 argmax parity on the mamba-130m probe. Fits a 7B in ~7.7GB on one 24GB L4, no OOM.
reference_verified.tokenizer_and_template: falcon-mamba-7b-tokenizer.gguf verified correct (gpt2, vocab 65024). ROOT-CAUSE of the historical garble: chatTemplate was 'zephyr' — its <|user|>/<|assistant|>/</s> markers are absent from the Falcon vocab and this is a BASE completion model, so the frame byte-fragmented into salad (falcon's FIRST special token is literally >>TITLE<<, which was the exact observed garbage). Fixed to chatTemplate:'raw' (passthrough, no role markers).

matmuls.backend: cuda_matmul.rs try_mat_vec_q8_0_kernel (raw-Q8 in-kernel dequant GEMV), shared with the transformer Q8 path (same math.rs entry)
matmuls.measured: in_proj [16384x4096] 0.45ms, out_proj [4096x8192] 0.31ms on one L4 — the matmuls are already GPU-fast

selective_scan.current: CPU S6 recurrence in mamba1_s6_step — THE tok/s bottleneck (7B falcon ~0.45 tok/s: matmuls GPU-fast, scan CPU-bound ~2.2s/token)
selective_scan.recurrence: per layer, over d_inner x d_state: h_t = dA_t (.) h_{t-1} + dB_t (.) x_t ; y_t = sum_state( C_t (.) h_t ) + D (.) x_t, with dA_t = exp(dt_t (.) A), dB_t = dt_t (.) B, dt_t = softplus(dt_proj . dt_src + bias); FalconMamba: RMSNorm(B), RMSNorm(C), RMSNorm(dt) (weightless, eps 1e-6) before discretization
selective_scan.kernel_plan: CUDA selective-scan: GPU-resident state h [d_inner x d_state] per layer; one thread-block per channel-tile, chunked/parallel scan across the token dimension for prefill and O(1) per-token step for decode; reuse the existing cuda_matmul.rs launcher + streams
selective_scan.correctness_gate: GPU-scan next-token argmax == CPU-scan argmax == this qspec's expect_token on prompt_tokens, AND a new `--gpu-selftest scan` mode PASSES with max_rel_err <= 1e-4 vs the CPU scan (mirrors flux-txt2img --gpu-selftest). No admission without parity.
selective_scan.perf_gate: warm decode >= 5 tok/s for falcon-mamba-7b on one L4 (up from 0.45), novel prompts, non-degenerate coherent output; mamba-2.8b >= 10 tok/s

perf.bottleneck: CPU selective-scan (the O(1)-state advantage is not yet on the GPU)

note: The 'Mistral-class SSM on one cheap L4' killshot. FalconMamba-7B (lighteval 64.09 > Mistral-7B 60.97) is a PURE SSM => O(1) recurrent state, O(n) long-prompt => edge-able fallthrough that moots the GPU. INFRA + CORRECTNESS PROVEN: loads + serves + Q8-fits on one L4; forward math HF-exact; reknot (intermediate_size 65536->8192), FalconMamba RMSNorms, entrypoint :8090 port-race + 300->900s health-timeout fixes, and chatTemplate zephyr->raw all landed. REMAINING = this qspec only: a GPU selective-scan kernel to take the 7B from 0.45 -> >=5 tok/s. Correctness is done; this is pure perf. Serving env: MESH_GPU_Q8K=1, MESH_GPU_Q8K_BUDGET_GB=16, MESH_CHILD_READY_TIMEOUT_MS=900000, FAT_STATION_HEALTH_TIMEOUT_S=900.
